title: Agrostophyllum stipulatum
taxonomy: Agrostophyllum stipulatum is a species within the plant family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the genus Agrostophyllum, which is a group of flowering plants native to the tropical regions of Asia. The classification of Agrostophyllum stipulatum places it within the subtribe Aeridinae, a subcategory of the tribe Epidendroideae. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics, such as its flower structure and growth habits. By placing Agrostophyllum stipulatum within this specific classification, botanists can better understand its evolutionary history and relationships to other orchid species.

intro: Agrostophyllum stipulatum, commonly known as the Himalayan orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various infections, wounds, and other health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Agrostophyllum stipulatum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments, making it a popular ingredient in natural medicine and skincare products.
